item.title: Kioxia Shares Awash in Buy Orders After AI-Driven Profit Surge
item.description: Kioxia Holdings Corp.’s shares were untraded in a glut of buy orders Monday morning after the supplier of storage for AI data centers reported soaring profit and gave an outlook that trounced expectations.

item.title: China Swap Connect Trading Volume Heads Toward 1 Trillion Yuan
item.description: Trading volumes through China’s Swap Connect are poised to accelerate as a growing appetite for yuan debt drives a surge in hedging demand, according to strategists.

item.title: Evergrande Liquidators Pursue Claims Against PwC in HK Court
item.description: China Evergrande’s liquidators head to court on Monday for the opening hearing of their lawsuit against PricewaterhouseCoopers International Ltd., in a case that will test the scope of claims against auditors of insolvent companies.

item.title: Elliott Builds Stake in Life-Science Firm Bio-Rad, WSJ Says
item.description: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has built a sizable stake in Bio-Rad Laboratories to boost the firm’s underperfoming stock price, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Korean Stocks Near Correction as Higher Yields Hurt Risk Demand
item.description: South Korea’s benchmark Kospi index fell, as rising bond yields threaten the world’s hottest stock rally driven by artificial intelligence.

item.title: Australians Expect Budget Will Leave Them Worse Off, Poll Shows
item.description: Australians broadly expect the Labor government’s annual budget will leave them worse off, according to a new opinion poll, as Treasurer Jim Chalmers’ efforts to increase fairness while tackling inflation failed to gain traction with the public.

item.title: Grains Jump After US Says China to Buy Billions in Farm Goods
item.description: Grain futures in Chicago jumped on Monday after the White House announced that China had agreed to buy at least $17 billion annually in American agricultural produce through 2028, following US President Donald Trump’s meetings in Beijing last week.

item.title: Jeweler Bao Tin Manh Hai Eyes Vietnam IPO as Gold Market Evolves
item.description: Bao Tin Manh Hai Jewelry JSC, one of Vietnam’s largest gold retailers, is preparing for an initial public offering in the fourth quarter as it pursues an aggressive expansion plan and seeks to capitalize on gold reforms aimed at opening up the market.

item.title: Australia Orders Chinese Investors to Sell Rare Earth Stakes
item.description: Australia’s government has ordered the biggest shareholders in rare earths firm Northern Minerals Ltd. to sell their stakes, the second such intervention in two years as Canberra seeks to protect the company from China-linked investors.

item.title: Sara Duterte Trial to Open Next Phase of Philippine Political Turmoil
item.description: After a tumultuous week that started with a Senate power grab and descended into a terrifying night of gunfire, the impeachment saga against Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte moves Monday to the trial phase.

item.title: Jane Street Doubles Capacity in Singapore as Expansion Rolls On
item.description: Jane Street Group LLC moved into a new Singapore office this month, doubling its seating capacity to 250 people as it continues to ramp up operations across Asia, said a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as Hormuz Quagmire Keeps Inflation Fear High
item.description: Gold held a decline as a lack of progress in reopening the Strait of Hormuz continued to fan inflation concerns that sent bond markets tumbling.

item.title: YouTube and Netflix Are Starting to Sound a Lot Like Normal TV
item.description: YouTube is pitching advertisers on a new slate of shows -- just like a TV network.

item.title: Oil Gains, Treasury Futures Slip on Iran Deadlock: Markets Wrap
item.description: Oil extended gains and Treasury futures slipped as the US and Iran remained deadlocked in efforts to end nearly three months of conflict and restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Extends Gains as Trump Says Clock Is Ticking for Iran Deal
item.description: Oil rose for a third day as the US and Iran remained far apart on a deal to end weeks of war and reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: German Finance Chief Urges Supply-Chain Resilience Ahead of G-7
item.description: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil urged partners to deepen cooperation on raw materials, energy and supply chains to reduce vulnerability in face of challenges such as the Iran war.

item.title: One Hedge Fund Holds the Keys to Ukraine’s Wartime Industries
item.description: VR Capital has built a powerful position in Ukrainian bonds, giving it enormous influence as companies key to the war effort try to restructure their debt.

item.title: NextEra Said to Discuss Paying About $76 Per Share for Dominion
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc. is discussing a mostly stock deal for Dominion Energy Inc. that would value the company at about $76 per share, or around $66 billion, making it by far the largest power deal on record, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Stocks in Focus After Bonds Send Inflation Alarm
item.description: With no clear-cut end in sight to the Iran war, investors enter a new week under the spell of its key economic consequence: rising global interest rates, and the inflation threat they signal.

item.title: US Says China Agrees to Spend Billions on Agricultural Goods
item.description: China has agreed to purchase at least $17 billion of agricultural products from the US annually through 2028, the White House said in a fact sheet detailing President Donald Trump’s two-day summit in China.

item.title: Gundlach Says It’s ‘Just Not Possible’ for the Fed to Cut Rates
item.description: Investors won’t see a rate cut out of the next Federal Reserve policy meeting, according to DoubleLine Capital LP chief executive officer Jeffrey Gundlach.

item.title: UK Nearing Free Trade Deal with Gulf Council States, FT Says
item.description: The UK and Gulf Cooperation Council states are close to finalizing a long-sought free trade deal after years of negotiations, the Financial Times reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple’s New ChatGPT-Like Siri App Will Have Auto-Deleting Chats
item.description: Also: A Genmoji upgrade is coming in iOS 27.

item.title: Tech Bubble Fear Lures Investors to Hedge With Exotic Options
item.description: The tech-fueled stock rally is looking bubble-like to some investors, and they’re turning to exotic options that better protect against an eventual slump.

item.title: Bond Traders See Tipping Point Toward New Era of Higher Yields
item.description: A new era of elevated borrowing costs is potentially underway as war-driven inflation angst intensifies in the US bond market, sending 30-year yields toward a two-decade high above 5%.

item.title: Volatile Chipmaker Stocks Emerge as Key Driver of S&P 500 Rally
item.description: Shares of chipmakers are increasingly dominating the S&P 500 Index, powering stocks to record highs while stirring concerns over the rally’s durability.

item.title: Emerging Carry Trade Rebounds With Real, Rand Among Favorites
item.description: The emerging-market carry trade has bounced back from its Iran war losses as surging crude oil prices reinforce expectations that interest rates will stay elevated and bolster the currencies of commodity exporters.

item.title: Malaysia’s Coalition Tensions Deepen as Anwar Floats Snap Poll
item.description: Fresh strains are emerging in Malaysia’s ruling alliance as political jockeying and oneupmanship among the coalition’s parties led Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim to raise the prospect of a snap general election if divisions deepen.

item.title: Israel’s Economy Slumps More Than Expected in the First Quarter
item.description: Israel’s economy slumped in the first quarter of the year when the fallout of the war with Iran imposed security-related shutdowns on businesses for more than a month.

item.title: Vietnam’s Largest Refinery Sees ‘Stable’ Operations Through June
item.description: Vietnam’s largest oil refinery continues to diversify crude supply and is confident that it can maintain stable operations through June, according to a company statement.
